name: Oxtails in Red Wine Sauce
recipeIngredient: 2 leeks (white and pale green parts only), chopped
5 cups chicken stock or low-salt canned broth
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat oil in a heavy large Dutch oven over high heat.
2. Season oxtails with salt and pepper. Add to the Dutch oven and cook until brown on all sides, about 12 minutes.
3. Transfer oxtails to a platter. Add celery, carrots, leeks, onion, and garlic to the pot and sauté until golden brown, about 8 minutes. Arrange oxtails over vegetables.
4. Add wine, thyme, parsley, cloves, and bay leaves. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat and cover Dutch oven tightly. Simmer until meat is very tender, about 1 1/2 hours.
5. Remove oxtails from cooking liquid. Tent with foil to keep warm. Strain the cooking liquid. Skim fat from the surface and reserve.
6. Transfer 2 tablespoons of skimmed fat to a heavy medium saucepan over medium heat. Add flour and stir until mixture browns, about 10 minutes.
7. Whisk in cooking liquid. Simmer until sauce thickens enough to coat back of spoon and is reduced to 2 cups, stirring frequently, about 45 minutes. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
8. Rewarm oxtails in sauce over low heat. Transfer to a large bowl and serve.
